question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted transaction with a consensusTimestamp just outside the dateRangeFilter window so that dateRangeFilter.filter() returns false, causing recordItemListener.onItem() to be skipped and the transfer amounts for that transaction to be permanently omitted from the database, leading to direct loss of funds visibility for affected accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that aggregator.accept(recordItem) is called for ALL items before the dateRangeFilter check, so that gasUsed and logsBloom in the persisted RecordFile include data from transactions that were never written to the database, causing the on-chain gas accounting to diverge from the actual persisted state and enabling incorrect fee refund or gas-limit bypass claims?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where a crafted v6 RecordFile with a manipulated block_number (index) field causes the offset calculation (recordFile.getIndex() - lastRecordFile.getIndex() - 1) to produce a large negative value, and repository.updateIndex(offset) then applies that negative offset to ALL rows in record_file with no WHERE clause, corrupting every block index in the database and breaking EVM transaction ordering for all historical fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the offset value (recordFile.getIndex() - lastRecordFile.getIndex() - 1) to overflow a long by supplying a v6 RecordFile whose index is Long.MAX_VALUE while lastRecordFile.getIndex() is a small positive number, resulting in a wrapped negative offset being applied globally to all record_file index values, permanently corrupting block numbers used for EVM fund transfer lookups?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractFunctionResult with getGasUsed() set to Long.MAX_VALUE so that the gasUsed += result.getGasUsed() accumulation in RecordItemAggregator overflows to a negative value, causing recordFile.setGasUsed() to store a negative gas total that downstream EVM fee calculations treat as zero or a refund, enabling direct loss of fee revenue?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that parserContext.addAll(recordFile.getSidecars()) is called unconditionally regardless of whether any transactions in the file passed the dateRangeFilter, causing sidecar contract state changes (including token balance mutations) to be persisted to the database without their corresponding parent transactions, creating phantom state changes that misrepresent fund ownership?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the @Retryable mechanism by causing a transient failure mid-parse, so that on retry the same RecordFile is re-processed after parserContext.clear() discards the partial state, resulting in duplicate onItem() calls for financial transactions that were already partially committed, leading to double-counted crypto transfers in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where the consensusTimestamp of a high-value transfer transaction exactly equals the dateRangeFilter boundary (start == timestamp), and due to the inclusive boundary check (timestamp >= start), the transaction is included, but a boundary-off-by-one in the filter construction causes it to be excluded, silently dropping the transfer record from the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared AtomicInteger logIndex being passed by reference to recordItem.setLogIndex(logIndex) before onItem() is called, so that if onItem() increments the logIndex internally, a subsequent recordItem in the same file receives the same log index, causing EVM event log deduplication to silently drop one of two transfer events with the same index?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where recordFile.getItems() returns items in non-consensus-timestamp order, causing the logIndex AtomicInteger to assign incorrect sequential indices to EVM transfer logs, breaking the log ordering invariant that downstream DeFi integrations rely on to reconstruct fund flows?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user cause applicationEventPublisher.publishEvent(new RecordFileParsedEvent(this, recordFile.getConsensusEnd())) to fire before any recordItem is processed, so that event listeners that act on this event and query the database for the newly parsed transactions find no data (TOCTOU), causing downstream financial reconciliation to treat the block as empty and skip fund transfer processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile containing a ContractFunctionResult where result.getBloom() returns a ByteString of length other than 256 bytes, causing logsBloom.or() to silently corrupt the bloom filter stored in the RecordFile, making subsequent bloom-filter-based event log queries return false negatives that hide fund transfer events from DeFi integrations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Transactional boundary combined with @Retryable so that a retry after a database timeout commits the first attempt's partial writes before the retry begins, causing the second attempt to re-insert the same crypto transfer rows, violating uniqueness constraints or silently duplicating fund transfer records depending on the upsert semantics of the underlying batch persister?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user cause getLast() to return a stale v5 RecordFile (from a previous parse cycle) while the current recordFile is v6, so that the offset is computed against the wrong baseline, and repository.updateIndex(offset) applies an incorrect global offset to all block indices, causing EVM block number lookups to return wrong transactions for fund transfer queries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile with zero items (empty getItems() list), causing recordFile.setCount(0) and aggregator.update() to set gasUsed=0 and logsBloom=empty, while parserContext.add(recordFile) still persists the empty RecordFile, creating a gap in the block chain that causes downstream block explorers to report missing fund transfers for that block range?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where all items have consensusTimestamps that fall outside the dateRangeFilter, so that count remains 0 and no transactions are persisted, but parserContext.add(recordFile) still marks the file as processed, permanently preventing re-processing of the file and causing all fund transfers in that file to be lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where a child transaction (isTopLevel() returns false) contains a ContractFunctionResult with a large gasUsed value, and since accept() returns early for non-top-level items, the gasUsed for that child is excluded from the RecordFile total, causing the persisted gasUsed to undercount actual gas consumption and enabling gas-limit manipulation in fee calculations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that recordFile.setCount(count.get()) only counts items that passed the dateRangeFilter, while aggregator.update(recordFile) sets gasUsed from ALL items, creating a mismatch between the count field and the gasUsed field in the persisted RecordFile that downstream gas-per-transaction calculations divide incorrectly, leading to wrong fee assessments?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the batch parse(List<RecordFile>) path where multiple RecordFiles are processed in a single @Transactional boundary, and a failure in processing the Nth file causes the entire batch to roll back, but the @Retryable mechanism retries only the failed file rather than the full batch, causing the first N-1 files to be re-processed and their fund transfers to be double-counted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the condition (lastRecordFile.getVersion() < VERSION && recordFile.getVersion() >= VERSION) to be satisfied multiple times across retries, so that repository.updateIndex(offset) is called multiple times with the same offset, cumulatively shifting all block indices by a multiple of the intended offset and corrupting the entire block index table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where recordItem.getConsensusTimestamp() returns Long.MIN_VALUE, causing dateRangeFilter.filter() to return false (since Long.MIN_VALUE < start), silently dropping the transaction, while aggregator.accept() still processes it and adds its gasUsed to the RecordFile total, creating an inconsistency between persisted transactions and the RecordFile's gas accounting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where getSidecars() returns sidecar files whose consensusEnd does not match any persisted transaction's consensusTimestamp, causing parserContext.addAll(recordFile.getSidecars()) to persist orphaned sidecar records that reference non-existent transactions, corrupting the contract state history used to reconstruct token balances?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized keyword on parse() being a Java monitor lock that is bypassed when parse() is called via a Spring AOP proxy (for @Transactional or @Retryable), allowing concurrent parse() invocations to process overlapping RecordFiles simultaneously and insert duplicate fund transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where the same consensusTimestamp appears on multiple RecordItems, causing the logIndex AtomicInteger to assign different indices to what should be the same transaction's logs, breaking the deduplication logic in downstream EVM event processors that use (blockNumber, logIndex) as a unique key for fund transfer events?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.update()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile with a ContractFunctionResult whose bloom field is a ByteString of exactly 0 bytes, causing DomainUtils.toBytes(result.getBloom()) to return an empty array, and logsBloom.or() to silently no-op, so that the bloom filter stored in the RecordFile omits real transfer events, causing bloom-filter-based event queries to return false negatives that hide fund movements?
